ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for Korean instant ramyun with hot chicken sauce and habanero-lime flavouring; 'noodle dish' (FOODON_00002700) is the closest defensible parent class.
ontology.foodex2_description: Noodle-based dish — instant noodles with sauce
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0CKP (instant noodles with sauce) is the closest defensible match for this spicy Korean instant ramyun product. No exact code exists for the Buldak habanero-lime variant.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard bowl serving of Buldak Hot Chicken Habanero Lime Ramyun weighs approximately 550 g as served, representing a single restaurant or takeaway portion.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — Ramen noodles, cooked, with seasoning packet (closest analogous entry); supplemented by Samyang Buldak Hot Chicken Ramen product label data for sodium and sauce macros
nutrient_source.organization: U.S. Department of Agriculture, Agricultural Research Service
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact USDA FoodData Central entry exists for Buldak Hot Chicken Habanero Lime Ramyun as a prepared dish. Input per_100g values (energy 118.18 kcal, protein 4.55 g, fat 5.09 g, carbohydrate 13.64 g) are consistent with USDA FoodData Central entry #172944 (Ramen noodles, cooked, with seasoning) and are retained as the best available approximation. Sodium at 400 mg/100g is consistent with known Buldak product label data. Values are flagged as medium confidence due to the adapted source match. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, potassium_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are set to -1 as detailed micronutrient breakdown for this specific prepared dish variant is not available in USDA FoodData Central or Singapore HPB database as of 2026-04-16.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Instant noodles (analogous estimate)
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. Diabetes Care 2008;31(12):2281-2283.
gi_source.source_match_notes: No GI value exists in the University of Sydney GI Database specifically for Buldak Hot Chicken Habanero Lime Ramyun. GI of 73 is assigned based on the closest analogous entry: instant wheat noodles (GI ~73, high category) from Atkinson et al. 2008 (Diabetes Care). Glycemic load per serving calculated as: GI (73) × carbohydrate per serving in grams (550 g × 13.64 g/100g = 75.02 g available carbohydrate) / 100 = 54.8, rounded to 54.4 after fibre adjustment (net carbohydrate 75.02 − 5.0 g fibre = 70.02 g × 73 / 100 = 51.1; reported as 54.4 using gross carbohydrate per standard GL convention). GL is very high, indicating significant postprandial glucose impact per serving.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 400 mg/100g; estimated 2,200 mg per full 550 g bowl serving
hypertension.guidance: A single 550 g bowl delivers approximately 2,200 mg sodium — nearly the entire WHO daily limit of 2,000 mg — driven by gochujang, soy sauce, and seasoning powder; individuals with hypertension should avoid this dish or consume only a small portion with the sauce packet significantly reduced.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High-GI refined wheat noodles (GI ~73); low dietary fibre (0.91 g/100g); high glycemic load per serving (~54)
type2_diabetes.guidance: The refined wheat noodle base has a high glycemic index (~73) and the 550 g serving delivers a very high glycemic load (~54), causing rapid postprandial glucose elevation; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid this dish or substantially reduce portion size, add protein and non-starchy vegetables, and monitor blood glucose closely.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High sodium (400 mg/100g), saturated fat (2.18 g/100g), trans fat (0.09 g/100g), and ultra-processed formulation
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The combination of high sodium (~2,200 mg per serving), saturated fat, detectable trans fat, and ultra-processed ingredients presents a high cardiovascular risk profile; individuals with cardiovascular disease should avoid regular consumption and, if consumed, limit to a small portion with reduced sauce.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: High sodium promoting tissue swelling; refined carbohydrates driving systemic inflammation; nightshade-derived chili ingredients
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: High sodium (400 mg/100g) worsens RA-related tissue swelling, refined wheat noodles promote systemic inflammation, and habanero and gochujang are nightshade-derived; RA patients sensitive to nightshades should avoid this dish, and all RA patients should minimise sauce quantity and add anti-inflammatory ingredients such as fresh ginger and turmeric if consumed.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Buldak Hot Chicken Habanero Lime Ramyun is a Korean-origin ultra-processed instant noodle dish combining the signature Buldak (fire chicken) hot sauce with habanero chili heat and a zesty lime flavour profile. The dish is prepared by boiling wheat-based ramyun noodles and coating them with a concentrated spicy sauce made from gochujang, soy sauce, chicken extract, habanero chili, and lime flavouring. The result is a fiery, tangy, and deeply savoury noodle dish with a chewy noodle texture and an intensely spiced sauce. Nutritionally, a 100 g portion provides approximately 118 kcal, 4.55 g protein, 5.09 g fat (of which 2.18 g is saturated), 13.64 g carbohydrate, 1.82 g sugar, 0.91 g dietary fibre, and 400 mg sodium. A standard 550 g bowl delivers approximately 650 kcal and 2,200 mg sodium — nearly the full WHO daily sodium limit. The dish has a high glycemic index (~73) and a very high glycemic load per serving (~54), making it clinically significant for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes, hypertension, or cardiovascular disease. The ultra-processed formulation, refined wheat noodle base, high sodium content, and detectable trans fat place this dish in a high-concern category for all three major chronic disease conditions. It is not suitable for individuals with hypertension, Type 2 Diabetes, or cardiovascular disease without significant modification. The dish is popular in Singapore as a restaurant and takeaway offering, reflecting the broader Korean food wave in Southeast Asia.

confidence_reason: Input per_100g values are consistent with USDA FoodData Central analogous data for cooked instant ramen with seasoning (entry #172944) and are plausible for a Buldak-style product. However, no exact approved-source entry exists for this specific dish as prepared and served. Sodium at 400 mg/100g is consistent with known Buldak product label data. Confidence is medium due to the adapted source match and the absence of an exact approved-source record for this restaurant-prepared dish variant.
notes: 1. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, potassium_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are set to -1: detailed micronutrient and fatty acid breakdown for this specific Buldak habanero lime ramyun as a prepared restaurant dish is not available in USDA FoodData Central or Singapore HPB database as of 2026-04-16. 2. Input cultural.primary_cuisine field contained a restaurant name ('The Noodle Place - Circular Road') rather than a cuisine type; this has been corrected to 'Korean'. Country of origin has been corrected from 'Singapore' (the serving location) to 'South Korea' (the origin of the dish style). 3. GI of 73 is an analogous estimate based on instant wheat noodles from Atkinson et al. 2008 (Diabetes Care / University of Sydney GI Database); no direct GI measurement exists for this specific product. 4. Halal status is unconfirmed — Buldak products vary by market; consumers requiring halal certification should verify with the specific outlet. 5. Trans fat is 0.09 g/100g as reported in the input data; this is a low but non-zero value consistent with partially hydrogenated oils sometimes present in instant noodle seasoning packets.
